name: Encina de San Silvestre - Ledesma - Puente Mocho – circular from Zafrón
name_original: Encina de San Silvestre - Ledesma - Puente Mocho – circular desde Zafrón
region_intro: Mountain biking around Ledesma, located in the province of Salamanca, Spain, offers diverse natural landscapes for outdoor activities. The region's elevation, ranging from 700 to 800 meters, contributes to a varied environment. It features unique "dehesa" landscapes, the scenic Tormes River, and the Valdelosa Cork Oak Forest, providing varied terrain for mountain bikers. The Ledesma ATB Centre supports the sport with a 100-kilometer network of marked trails across five signposted routes, catering to various difficulty levels. Best mountain bike trails around Ledesma The most popular mountain bike trail is Zamarón - Calzada de Valdunciel – circular por Valdelosa y la Vía de la Plata, a 28.6 miles (46.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 34 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the Valdelosa area and parts of the Vía de la Plata. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Encina de San Silvestre - Ledesma - Puente Mocho – circular from Zafrón, a moderate 24.0 miles (38.7 km) path. This route offers views of the Ledesma Bridge and the Tormes River. Local mountain bikers also love the Peñausende — circular por la Vía Verde de la Plata, a 29.5 miles (47.4 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 48 minutes. Mountain biking around Ledesma is defined by rolling "dehesa" landscapes, routes along the Tormes River, and paths through cork oak forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, including dedicated ATB Centre trails. The routes in Ledesma are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 50 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Ledesma's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Ledesma?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails around Ledesma, catering to various skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes more than 50 times, with an average rating of 4.5 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Ledesma?
A: Mountain biking in Ledesma features diverse natural landscapes. You'll encounter rolling "dehesa" wooded pasturelands, scenic routes along the Tormes River, and paths winding through the Valdelosa Cork Oak Forest. The region's elevation, ranging from 700 to 800 meters, provides varied and engaging terrain.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Ledesma?
A: Ledesma offers a range of difficulty levels. You'll find 1 easy trail, 19 moderate trails, and 2 difficult trails, ensuring options for beginners to experienced riders. The Ledesma ATB Centre also provides a 100-kilometer network of marked trails distributed across five signposted routes, catering to various difficulties.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or natural sights to see along the mountain bike trails in Ledesma?
A: Yes, many routes offer views of historical and natural attractions. You can cycle past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5574008" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ledesma Bridge&lt;/a&gt; spanning the Tormes River, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4859387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mocho&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable sights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334852" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fortaleza - Ledesma Castle&lt;/a&gt; and various churches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3513669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zamayón Church&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5902010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa Maria la Mayor&lt;/a&gt;. The unique "dehesa" landscape and the Valdelosa Cork Oak Forest are also prominent natural features.

Q: Are there facilities available for mountain bikers in Ledesma?
A: Yes, the Ledesma ATB Centre is a significant resource for mountain bikers. It features a reception point that includes a bicycle repair shop, rental services, and bike washing facilities. Additionally, guided mountain biking tours are available from the center.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that are circular?
A: Many of the mountain bike routes around Ledesma are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994101848/zamaron-calzada-de-valdunciel-circular-por-valdelosa-y-la-via-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zamarón - Calzada de Valdunciel – circular por Valdelosa y la Vía de la Plata&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282104855/penausende-circular-por-la-via-verde-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñausende — circular por la Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Ledesma?
A: Yes, Ledesma offers at least one easy mountain bike trail suitable for families or beginners. The diverse network also includes many moderate routes that can be enjoyed by families with some biking experience, especially those exploring the dedicated ATB Centre trails.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Ledesma?
A: The milder weather during spring and autumn makes these seasons particularly recommended for enjoying Ledesma's outdoor offerings. The temperatures are generally more comfortable for active pursuits than the heat of summer or the chill of winter.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Ledesma?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on mountain bike trails can vary, many outdoor areas in Spain are dog-friendly, especially in natural settings like the "dehesa" landscapes. It's always advisable to keep dogs under control, on a leash where required, and to clean up after them to ensure a pleasant experience for all trail users and to protect local wildlife.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Ledesma?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, the scenic routes along the Tormes River, and the unique "dehesa" landscapes that define the region's mountain biking experience.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes in the Ledesma area?
A: Yes, Ledesma offers several longer routes for those seeking a challenge. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19807908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Peñausende – Wind Turbines on Teso Santo loop from Peñausende&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 38.5-mile (62 km) trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282165115/castillo-de-asmesnal-and-alfaraz-de-sayago-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Asmesnal and Alfaraz de Sayago Loop&lt;/a&gt; covers 32.6 miles (52.5 km).

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that follow historical paths?
A: Yes, the Ledesma ATB Centre highlights that enthusiasts can explore historical routes on their bikes. The region's rich medieval history means many paths have historical significance, offering a unique blend of sport and cultural exploration.
tips: Ledesma still preserves a large part of the granite stone wall that has historically surrounded the Villa in its entirety. Most of what remains today was built in the time of Fernando II de León, in the 12th century, although it was in the 15th century when several canvases with well-carved masonry were renovated.

This bridge, cataloged as an Asset of Cultural Interest in the category of Historic-Artistic Monument together with the remains of the Roman road at its ends, is considered by specialists to be of Roman origin, although from those times some think that only the site and the foundations that support it.
